src/flit/platform/system_state

  • Index
Search:
Group by:
  • Types
    • BatteryState
    • NetworkType
  • Procs
        batteryLevel
      • batteryLevel(): float32
        batteryState
      • batteryState(): BatteryState

        isOnline
      • isOnline(): bool
        networkType
      • networkType(): NetworkType

Battery and network state. Platform-specific queries that return cached snapshots; refresh via the explicit refresh* procs.

  • macOS: pmset shell helper for battery, scutil --dns plus route get for network
  • Linux: /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/, nmcli
  • Windows: WMI Win32_Battery, Get-NetConnectionProfile

All procs return sensible defaults when no battery / network exists (desktop without battery returns 100% / charging).

Types

BatteryState = enum
  bsUnknown, bsCharging, bsDischarging, bsFull, bsNoBattery
Power source / charge state for the active battery. Desktop machines without a battery report bsNoBattery; isCharging treats that as charging since there's no risk of running out. 
NetworkType = enum
  ntUnknown, ntNone, ntEthernet, ntWifi, ntCellular
Active network interface category. Reported best-effort; some platforms can't distinguish Wi-Fi from Ethernet and return ntUnknown.

Procs

proc batteryLevel(): float32 {....raises: [],
                               tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect],
                               forbids: [].}
Returns battery level as a fraction [0, 1]. 1.0 if no battery (desktop). On error or unknown, returns 1.0. 
proc batteryState(): BatteryState {....raises: [], tags: [ExecIOEffect,
    ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Returns whether the device is plugged in, on battery, etc. 
proc isCharging(): bool {....raises: [],
                          tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect],
                          forbids: [].}
Convenience: true if the device is plugged in (or has no battery, like a desktop). 
proc isOnline(): bool {....raises: [],
                        tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect],
                        forbids: [].}
True when any network interface has a default route. 
proc networkType(): NetworkType {....raises: [], tags: [ExecIOEffect, ReadIOEffect,
    RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Returns the active connection type. Polls quickly; doesn't maintain a watch.
